title: Facebook, troppa visibilità a estremisti e complottisti: ecco come impatta Meta sulle elezioni
snippet: Published on January 9, 2026, this article in 'La Discussione' cites a study from the University of Urbino, authored by Professor Fabio Giglietto. The study found that Facebook's algorithm penalizes institutional political visibility while promoting anti-European, pro-Russian, and conspiratorial extremist content.
description: This article from La Discussione cites Professor Fabio Giglietto's study from the University of Urbino, which found that Facebook's algorithm promotes extremist content while penalizing institutional political visibility.

title: lumsanews.it
description: This article from Lumsanews reports on an analysis by Professor Fabio Giglietto, asserting that Meta's social media platforms provided excessive visibility to extremists and conspiracy theorists, influencing electoral outcomes. It highlights his expertise in social media's impact on political discourse and information disorder.

title: Facebook premia gli estremisti: la ricerca dell'Università di Urbino - Lumsanews

title: Secondo uno studio dell'università di Urbino l'algoritmo di Facebook ora punisce la politica ufficiale e premia gli estremisti antieuropei. Pd e FdI verso una legge bipartisan sulle piattaforme - Open
snippet: This article from 'Open' on January 7, 2026, highlights a study by Professor Fabio Giglietto of the University of Urbino. The research, published on January 7, analyzes the effects of Meta's algorithmic choices on the distribution and visibility of political content, concluding that official political visibility on Facebook has plummeted while anti-European, pro-Russian, and conspiratorial extremisms have advanced. Giglietto is quoted on the study's findings.
description: An article from Open discusses a study by Professor Fabio Giglietto of the University of Urbino, published on January 7, 2026, analyzing how Facebook's algorithm impacts the distribution of political content and the visibility of extremist views.
